laws.title: Public Law 16-122 | The Bill Transparency Understanding Act
laws.status_basis: The supplied enactment text is unrecoverable. The source file is only a Google Drive permission-denial page, so no operative language can be authenticated or codified.

provisions.text_summary: No recoverable statutory text; only access-denied viewer debris.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only a source-limited historical note; do not claim a substantive repeal or codification outcome.
provisions.evidence: Google Drive message: "Only the owner and editors can download this file."
provisions.notes: The current XML note is source-limited, but its repeal/supersession label is unsupported.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML has a short statutory note at Title 2 section 1, but the source itself is unavailable, so the current repeal/supersession language is not grounded.

recommended_actions: Retain only a source-limited historical note.
Remove the unsupported repeal/supersession characterization.
Re-acquire the authentic source before any substantive codification.

laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.status_basis: The full source is present and the act is a substantive, generally applicable law-enforcement statute. It does not repeal itself, and no later repeal appears in the supplied materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and acronym.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short historical note only.
provisions.evidence: The act is referred to as the Law Enforcement Accountability Act or LEAA.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and anti-supersession clause.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as savings language only.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent.

provisions.text_summary: Effective on passage and signature.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short effective-date note.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be effective upon passage and signature of the President.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of apprehension, bite, canine handler, force levels, and related terms.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new permanent definitions section for the law-enforcement framework.
provisions.evidence: SEC4.DEFINITIONS For the purpose of this bill -
provisions.notes: Substantive permanent material; not merely a note.

provisions.text_summary: Rules for tactical and non-tactical canine deployment, call-off duties, and handler training.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new permanent canine-operations section.
provisions.evidence: Canine deployments can be authorised for the following assignments.
provisions.notes: The source also contains a separate SEC5 use-of-force block.

provisions.text_summary: Objective-reasonableness, de-escalation, proportional force, deadly-force limits, medical aid, and intervention/reporting duties.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new permanent use-of-force section with the supplied standards.
provisions.evidence: law enforcement shall only use force that is objectively reasonable.

provisions.text_summary: Attorney General and agency leadership enforcement directive.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new enforcement section or preserve the directive as a short note attached to the new Title 18 material.
provisions.evidence: The Attorney General of the United States ... shall enact policies and procedures to enforce the provisions of this act.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML is only a short note at Title 18 section 1. It does not carry the substantive canine and use-of-force rules as real Code text.

recommended_actions: Codify the substantive rules as real permanent sections instead of a bare note.
Keep only the short-title, severability, and effective-date material as note content.
Remove the Trello link from the current note.

laws.title: Public Law 17-125 | We Don't Read Minds Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It imposes monthly Inspector General reporting duties and penalties for noncompliance, and the supplied materials do not show a repeal of the act itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short historical note only.
provisions.evidence: This act may also be referred to as the We Don't Read Minds Act.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose to require monthly updates to Congress.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as purpose language only.
provisions.evidence: The purpose of this act is to mandate monthly updates to Congress.

provisions.text_summary: Each executive department's Inspector General must submit monthly reports to Congress.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new permanent reporting-duty section for Inspector Generals.
provisions.evidence: the Inspector General from each executive department must be tasked with delivering and submitting monthly reports

provisions.text_summary: House inquiry authority and fines for non-reporting.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new enforcement and penalty subsection tied to the reporting duty.
provisions.evidence: the OIG being fined of up to $10,000 in USD and any further punishment deemed necessary by the House

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately after passage and presidential signature.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short effective-date note.
provisions.evidence: This bill will take effect immediately after passage through both houses of Congress and a signature from the President.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is anchored in Title 5 section 101 and incorrectly labels the law repealed or superseded. The source supports a live reporting mandate.

recommended_actions: Keep the act as a live Title 5 reporting statute.
Replace the repeal label with a current-status treatment.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-126 | Special Events Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition trail classifies this enactment as superseded before codification, with PL-031-226 later repealing Public Law 17-126 in full. The current XML note is therefore a historical artifact, not live Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and acronym.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short historical note only.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be referred to as the Special Events Act or SEA.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and anti-supersession clause.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as savings language only.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent.

provisions.text_summary: Effective on passage and signature.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as a short effective-date note.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be effective upon passage and signature of the President.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of permit, special event, and forgery.
provisions.exact_change: Retain only as a local/D.C. historical note if needed; do not convert to permanent Code text.
provisions.evidence: For the purpose of this bill - "Special Event" means ... within the district of columbia.

provisions.text_summary: Department of Commerce and Labor permit authority, database, notice, and denial rules.
provisions.exact_change: Exclude from permanent Code text; keep only as local history.
provisions.evidence: The Department of Commerce and Labor shall have the power to issue permits for special events within the district of columbia.

provisions.text_summary: Forgery penalties, suspension/blacklist authority, and enforcement rules.
provisions.exact_change: Exclude from permanent Code text; keep only as local history.
provisions.evidence: Any forgery of a Special Events Permit shall be classed as a misdemeanour.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is parked in Title 50 and labels the law repealed or superseded. The source is a District-of-Columbia special-events statute, so the anchor and status are both off.

recommended_actions: Do not treat this as Title 50 material.
Keep only a concise local-history note if one is retained at all.
Do not create operative Code text for this law.
Remove the unsupported live-law framing from the note.

laws.title: Public Law 17-127 | Border Security Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and contains a blanket Title 8 repeal followed by multiple express rewrites of Title 8 sections. It remains operative in the source chain and is not itself repealed.

provisions.text_summary: General provision invoking Public Law 1-1 applicability conventions.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only as a short explanatory note.
provisions.evidence: Pursuant to Public Law 1-1, codes listed in this act shall be applicable section(s).

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Title 8 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Mark Title 8 as repealed, then carry forward the rewritten sections from later subsections.
provisions.evidence: U.S. Code: Title 8 is repealed in its entirety.
provisions.notes: This is followed by express reenactments and rewrites.

provisions.exact_change: Replace each named section with the supplied replacement text.
provisions.evidence: 8 U.S.Code 1101 shall now read as ... 8 U.S.Code 1551 shall now read as ...
provisions.notes: These are the core immigration-definition and DHS-organization rewrites.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites sections 1182 and 1189 with alien-inadmissibility and foreign-terrorist-organization rules.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the two sections with the supplied replacement text.
provisions.evidence: 8 U.S.Code 1182 shall read as ... 8 U.S.Code 1189 shall now read as ...

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites sections 1532, 1533, 1534, and 1481.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the named sections with the supplied removal/procedure/nationality text.
provisions.evidence: 8 U.S.Code 1532 shall now read as ... 8 U.S.Code 1481 shall now read as ...
provisions.notes: PL 19-147 later repeals section 1102, so sequencing must account for the later repeal.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML contains only a note attached deep inside section 1101. The act requires actual textual amendments and a repeal-marking chain, not just a note.

recommended_actions: Apply the express section rewrites to Title 8 sections 1101, 1551, 1102, 1103, 1105, 1182, 1189, 1532, 1533, 1534, and 1481.
Mark the Title 8 repeal in sequence with the reenactments.
Account for the later repeal of section 1102 by PL 19-147.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-128 | Impeachment Procedures and Protocols Act (IPPA)
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and contains congressional-procedure rules plus a determinable amendment to 28 U.S. Code 1365. It is not self-repealed, and the supplied materials do not show a later repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as short note material only.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be referred to as Impeachment Procedures and Protocols Act or IPPA.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions and impeachment-inquiry / impeachment / conviction procedures.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as congressional-procedure note or Title 2 material, not as a bare repeal note.
provisions.evidence: The committee ... shall have the authority to issue subpoenas ... and the Senate shall immediately start the process of the impeachment trial.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 28 U.S. Code 1365 on congressional subpoena enforcement.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 1365 with the supplied enforcement text.
provisions.evidence: 28 U.S.Code 1365 shall now read as - 28 U.S.Code 1365 - Congressional actions

provisions.text_summary: Compliance duties and rule-of-construction preserving congressional authority.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as procedural note material or Title 2 sections.
provisions.evidence: Nothing in this Act may be interpreted to limit or constrain Congress's inherent authority.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML note is just a Title 2 history note and does not perform the required 28 U.S.C. 1365 amendment.

recommended_actions: Amend 28 U.S.C. 1365 with the SEC9 replacement text.
Preserve the impeachment-procedure material as Title 2 note or new sections.
Remove the unsupported repeal/supersession label.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-129 | Department of Education Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and supplies an organic-act style Department of Education framework. It is a live enactment, not a repeal, and its substantive provisions are permanent.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, findings, and purposes.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as note material only.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Department of Education Act.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions, establishment of the department, principal officers, and the Office of Career and Technical Education.
provisions.exact_change: Codify as new permanent Title 20 sections.
provisions.evidence: There is established an executive department to be known as the Department of Education.
provisions.notes: This is the core organic-act material.

provisions.text_summary: Transfers from Commerce and Labor and Justice, plus administrative provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the transfers and administrative authority as Title 20 operative text or a tightly trimmed note chain.
provisions.evidence: There shall be transferred to the Secretary ... all functions of the Secretary of Commerce and Labor relating to such education.

provisions.text_summary: Construction, severability, and reference/savings language.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as savings language attached to the Title 20 enactment.
provisions.evidence: Any provision of this Act held to be invalid ... shall be deemed severable.

provisions.text_summary: Effective date and interim appointments.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as an effective-date note.
provisions.evidence: This bill will take effect immediately after passage through both houses of Congress.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note carries a full quoted-content dump and still labels the law repealed or superseded. The source supports a real organic-act style Title 20 enactment, so the dump is inappropriate.

recommended_actions: Codify the organic-act text as real Title 20 sections.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link from the current note.
Drop the unsupported repeal/supersession label.

laws.title: Public Law 17-130 | Oath and Offices Protection (OOP)
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and creates a substantive oath-timing rule tied to 5 U.S. Code 3331. It does not repeal itself and should not be treated as Title 50 material.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as note material only.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be referred to as Oath and Offices Protection or OOP.

provisions.text_summary: General provision and definition tying 'oath of office' to 5 U.S.C. 3331.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new Title 5 cross-reference section near 3331.
provisions.evidence: The term 'oath of office' refers to the oath established in 5 U.S.Code 3331.

provisions.text_summary: Oath timing rules for elected, appointed, and acting officers; forfeiture for failure; President and Vice President excluded.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the oath-timing rule as a new permanent Title 5 section.
provisions.evidence: Failure to be sworn in within the allotted timeframe shall result in said person forfeiting their position or office.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML note is misanchored in Title 50 and falsely marked repealed. The source is a live Title 5 oath-timing statute.

recommended_actions: Move the substantive treatment to Title 5 near section 3331.
Drop the unsupported repeal label.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-132 | Standard Process for International Extraditions Act
laws.status_basis: The full source is present and creates a substantive extradition process with treaty limits and DOJ/State coordination. It does not itself repeal any Code text and it is not self-repealing.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and introductory definition block.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as short note material only.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Standard Process for International Extraditions Act.

provisions.text_summary: Definition of extradition.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new definition section or note describing extradition.
provisions.evidence: Extradition shall be defined as the removal of a person ... for criminal prosecution or punishment.

provisions.text_summary: Standard extradition processing rules, treaty/dual-criminality limits, non-citizen exception, and State/Justice certification.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as a permanent extradition-process section or closely related statutory note.
provisions.evidence: In accordance to 18 U.S.Code 3182, the extradition process shall require the following.
provisions.notes: The act references 18 U.S.C. 3182 but does not itself rewrite it.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is misanchored in Title 50. The source is an extradition statute tied to Title 18, so the existing placement is wrong.

recommended_actions: Move the enactment away from Title 50 and into a Title 18-appropriate placement or a close statutory note anchor.
Preserve the treaty and certification rules as permanent operative history.
Remove the Trello link.
